channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Activist Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike after 26 days amid political demands
item.description: Activist Sonam Wangchuk, who had been on a 26-day hunger strike, ended his fast at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram with the presence of Union ministers Jitendra Singh and JP Nadda. The protest, led by th...

item.title: China's Plan to Deflect Asteroids with High-Speed Spacecraft
item.description: China's National Space Administration is planning a planetary defense test that aims to alter an asteroid's trajectory by striking it with a spacecraft traveling at Mach 26. This mission, set for 2029...

item.title: Andy Burnham's Devolution Plan to Revitalize the UK Economy
item.description: The article outlines Andy Burnham's initiative, 'No 10 North,' aimed at decentralizing power and fostering regional growth across the UK. It critiques decades of centralization and deindustrialisation...

item.title: DepEd and LGUs Collaborate to Address Nationwide Classroom Shortage
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) has partnered with all local government units (LGUs) to tackle the nationwide classroom shortage, estimated at 144,000 units. Undersecretary Filemon Ray Javier emph...

item.title: PM Modi Announces Stricter Measures Against Paper Leaks and New Bill
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has announced stricter measures against examination paper leaks, including the introduction of a new bill to be discussed in the Union Cabinet. The government aims to esta...

item.title: Tanzania's Peace Initiatives and Tourism Boost
item.description: The article highlights two key developments in Tanzania. First, the African Alliance for Freedom and Progress (AAFP) has called for national unity and peace to drive the country's development. This co...

item.title: PM Modi Announces Stricter Measures Against Paper Leaks Amid CJP Protests
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has announced stricter measures against paper leaks during a cabinet meeting, addressing concerns over the NEET-UG 2026 exam leak that affected over 22 lakh students. The ...

item.title: Zebrafish Regenerate Gut Neurons in Nine Days, Offering Clues for Nerve Repair
item.description: Researchers at Michigan State University have developed a groundbreaking method to study nerve regeneration by observing zebrafish regenerate damaged gut neurons within nine days. The study, published...

item.title: Ryan Clark Fired from ESPN's NFL Live Show After Dismissal During Broadcast
item.description: Ryan Clark, a former NFL safety turned ESPN analyst, faced controversy after being abruptly dismissed from ESPN's 'NFL Live' show during a live broadcast. The incident sparked public speculation, with...

item.title: Kaduna Governorship Aspirant Resigns From ADC, Sparks Political Uncertainty Ahead Of 2027 Elections
item.description: Muhammad Bello, a former Kaduna State Commissioner for Education and prominent ADC governorship aspirant, has resigned from the African Democratic Congress (ADC), dealing a significant blow to the par...

item.title: BOSA terminates membership of Gauteng MPL Ayanda Allie following disciplinary process
item.description: Build One South Africa (BOSA) has officially terminated the membership of Ayanda Allie, its sole representative in the Gauteng Provincial Legislature. The decision followed an internal disciplinary pr...

item.title: PGA of America removes golf chief following Rory McIlroy abuse scandal and car crash incident
item.description: The PGA of America has removed its golf chief following a controversy involving Rory McIlroy and a car crash incident during a Ryder Cup interview. The decision comes amid allegations of abuse directe...

item.title: US imposes 12.5% tariffs on Australian goods under human rights pretext
item.description: The United States has increased tariffs on Australian products to 12.5 per cent, citing concerns over forced labour in supply chains. This decision follows a determination by the US trade office that ...

item.title: Key Stories: RCMP Mystery, Organ Donation Ethics, and Political Commentary
item.description: The article compiles several significant news stories. First, it explores the decades-long mystery of RCMP officer Joe Atherton, whose ashes vanished after his death in 1988. Second, it discusses the ...

item.title: Nigeria Cup 2026: Celebrating Tradition and Elevating Golf Standards
item.description: The Nigeria Cup 2026 is set to become a landmark event in Nigerian golf, blending tradition with modern excellence. Organized by the Premium Times, the tournament will take place from July 1st to July...

item.title: Deputy Finance Minister Masondo Resigns as PIC Chair Amid Governance Crisis
item.description: Deputy Finance Minister David Masondo has resigned as chairperson of the Public Investment Corporation (PIC), following the departure of six non-executive directors over the past week. The PIC, which ...

item.title: Global Markets Tumble as Oil Prices Surge Past $US100
item.description: Global equity markets faced significant declines as oil prices surged past the $US100 per barrel mark, sparking fears of inflation and triggering a sell-off on Wall Street. The rise in crude oil price...

item.title: Barrick and Government Boost Local Content in Mining Sector
item.description: Barrick Mining Corporation Tanzania has reaffirmed its commitment to supporting Tanzanians through local content initiatives under the Twiga Minerals joint venture with the government. As Tanzania aim...

item.title: The 1989 Pontiac Stinger: A Bold 80s Concept Car with Unmatched Innovation
item.description: The 1989 Pontiac Stinger stands as a quintessential example of 1980s automotive excess and creativity. This concept car, unveiled by General Motors, defied conventional design norms with its audacious...

item.title: Marcos Visits Injured Navy Personnel and Awards Order of Lapulapu in West Philippine Sea Incident
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. recently visited two Philippine Navy personnel injured during a confrontation with the Chinese Coast Guard in the West Philippine Sea. During the visit, Marcos personall...
